diffCoverage.notes: Threat-modeled the path source, the webview boundary, and the live-smoke output.

findings.evidence: Moving credential reading into the core is a net security improvement: the previous paste-JSON path put the bearer token into the webview DOM, whereas now the token is read from disk by Rust and only non-secret DTOs (CredentialSummary lengths/counts, AccountProfile id/name) cross IPC. The dev path is fixed (env or repo-root constant) and not UI-supplied, so no arbitrary-file-read via IPC. The attested live-smoke sidecar was scanned and contains no token values.
findings.fix: For release, source credentials from a user file picker + OS keychain (secure-cred-store) rather than a repo-root file, and clear them from memory on logout.

findings.evidence: read_dev_credentials validates required fields and returns a generic 'no session file found' message on read failure — no path internals or token values are leaked in errors.
findings.fix: Keep error messages value-free as the keychain flow is added.

summary: Security review: the change keeps bearer tokens out of the webview by reading .zalo-cred.json in the core; only non-secret DTOs cross IPC, the path is not UI-controllable, errors are value-free, and the attested smoke sidecar is secret-free. This is a dev affordance to be replaced by keychain-backed loading. No blocking findings.
